title: Generosity Feels Good in My Body
subtitle: An affirmation for building a physical sense of ease around giving
excerpt: A short affirmation for building a somatic relationship with generosity: the feeling in the chest when you give freely, rather than the tightness that comes with reluctance. The affirmation names what it feels like in the body, not just in principle.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 54 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use when you're about to give something, or after you've given and you want to anchor the good feeling.

content.text: A brief affirmation for building a bodily association between generosity and ease. The statement names what's true when giving comes from a full rather than a fearful place: it feels good, not costly.
The affirmation connects generosity to a physical sense of warmth and ease, rather than to a calculation. It names the feeling first, so the body registers it before the mind checks the balance.
Generosity feels good in my body. I give from fullness. Giving is part of the wealth I am building.
Say it out loud three times, hand on the centre of your chest. Say it just before you tip, donate, or give a gift. Let the warm feeling in your chest be the confirmation.
Adapted from Day 54 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
